items.q: Which colours typically advance in a composition?
items.why: Students conflate darkness with advancement. In atmospheric perspective, COOLER colours recede (think distant blue mountains) and warmer colours advance, regardless of darkness.

items.q: What did Pollock mean by his drip paintings being 'in the painting'?
items.correct: He worked from all sides, making the act of painting central to the meaning.
items.misconception: He literally fell into wet paint.
items.why: Students take 'in the painting' literally. Pollock meant his BODILY ENGAGEMENT - working around and over the canvas on the floor - was integral to the work, not just the marks left behind.

items.correct: The small projecting line at the end of letter strokes.
items.misconception: A whole typeface that looks formal.
items.why: Students conflate the FEATURE (the small foot/stroke) with the CLASSIFICATION (serif typefaces, which have them). Sans serif means 'without serifs' - those little projections at letter endings.

items.q: What is iconography the study of?
items.why: It's Symbols and their meanings. “Brush techniques” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: "A dove represents peace" describes which kind of meaning?
items.why: Correct answer: Connotation. “Denotation” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A comparative analysis typically discusses how many works at once?
items.why: Exact answer: 2. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
